{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8278208?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/AzureADTrent", "id": 8278208, "login": "AzureADTrent", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjgyNzgyMDg=", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/AzureADTrent", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 1.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:A/VC:N/VI:L/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:U"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-640", "name": "Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password"}], "description": "### Summary

The LoginLink signature used for password reset URLs covers only the user's `id` — it does not include the password hash. After a user clicks a reset link and successfully changes their password, the same link remains valid for up to 2 additional uses within a 1-hour window. Anyone who intercepts or caches the original link can log in as the user even after the password has been changed.

### Details

`config/packages/security.yaml:73-78` configures the login link:

```yaml
login_link:
    check_route: link_login_check
    signature_properties: ['id']
    lifetime: 900
    max_uses: 3
```

The HMAC signature covers only `id`, not `password`, `username`, or `email`. The effective lifetime for password reset links is extended to 3600 seconds by `PasswordResetController.php:106-119` via `getPasswordResetRetryLifetime()`.

After the first successful use (where the user sets a new password), the `__pw_reset__` session flag is cleared by `WizardController.php:105`. Subsequent uses of the same link bypass the forced-password-reset wizard and grant a normal authenticated session.

Symfony's `LoginLinkHandler::consumeLoginLink` decrements a per-link counter but allows up to 3 total uses. Re-issuing a new link does not invalidate the old one.

The same vulnerability affects `UserLoginLinkCommand.php:77`, which generates admin-on-demand login links with the same signature scheme.

### PoC

**Step 1 — Generate a password reset link:**

```bash
docker exec <kimai_container> bin/console kimai:user:login-link <username>
```

**Step 2 — Use the link to log in and change the password.**

**Step 3 — Open the same link in a different browser or incognito session.**

The link logs the attacker in as a fully authenticated user, despite the password having been changed in Step 2.

**Step 4 — The link works a third time (3 total uses within 1 hour).**

### Impact

A password reset link that leaks through any of the following channels remains a valid authentication credential even after the legitimate user has changed their password: corporate mail-relay scanners that pre-click links (Microsoft Defender ATP, Mimecast, Barracuda), shared inboxes, browser history sync across devices, HTTP referrer headers, or proxy/WAF logs. The user believes they have secured their account by setting a new password, but the leaked link still grants full access for up to 2 additional uses within the hour.

# Solution

Login links now also use the `password` hash for generating the signature, which will immediately expire the link after the user changed the password.

Read <https://www.kimai.org/en/security/ghsa-m492-gv72-xvxj> for more information.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-m492-gv72-xvxj", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-01T19:49:24Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m492-gv72-xvxj", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-m492-gv72-xvxj"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-01T19:49:24Z", "references": ["https://github.com/kimai/kimai/security/advisories/GHSA-m492-gv72-xvxj", "https://www.kimai.org/en/security/ghsa-m492-gv72-xvxj", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m492-gv72-xvxj"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/kimai/kimai/security-advisories/GHSA-m492-gv72-xvxj", "severity": "low", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/kimai/kimai", "summary": "Kimai Password Reset Link Remains Valid After Password Change", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-01T19:49:27Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-m492-gv72-xvxj", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.58.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "kimai/kimai"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 2.57.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Kimai Password Reset Link Remains Valid After Password Change
